metadata.set_name: The Lord of the Rings: Tales of Middle-earth
metadata.credit: Data and analysis provided by Draftsim
metadata.author: Bryan Hohns (Veveil)

set_overview.summary: LTR is one of the weakest and most poorly balanced sets in recent years. Black significantly outperforms other colors while green ranks near the bottom. The set plays aggressively due to "The Ring Tempts You", amass mechanics, plentiful combat tricks, and landcycling options that enable low land counts (14-15 lands possible).

color_notes.{B} Black: Strongest color by a considerable margin. Premium commons (Claim the Precious, Dunland Crebain, The Torment of Gollum).
color_notes.{U} Blue: Well-balanced with consistent power. Glorious Gale, Lórien Revealed, Pelargir Survivor.
color_notes.{R} Red: Highly uneven. Strong cards (Smite the Deathless, Rally at the Hornburg) contrast with traps (Gimli's Fury, Gimli's Axe).
color_notes.{W} White: Mediocre with inconsistent quality. Errand-Rider of Gondor and Eastfarthing Farmer are the best commons.
color_notes.{G} Green: Nearly unplayable in isolation. Only draft when pursuing strong Food or Elf synergies with premium uncommons/rares.

key_gameplay_notes: Aggressive format — combat-heavy with combat tricks and skulk creating predictable blocks.
Uncommon quality significantly exceeds common quality — follow premium signposts early.
Landcyclers enable 14-15 land counts in aggressive shells.
The Ring incentivizes consistent attack patterns.
Block skulk creatures with 1/3s and 0/3s effectively.

mechanics.name: The Ring Tempts You
mechanics.summary: Progresses through four levels. Level 1 (skulk) is unreliable; level 2 gains traction (enables attacks); level 3 (sac mode) underwhelms; level 4 threatens a real clock but lacks consistency. Ideal ringbearers are evasive 1/X creatures.

mechanics.name: Amass Orcs
mechanics.summary: Creates or augments Orc armies, providing aggressive pressure and tribal synergies with lords and Orc payoffs.

key_examples.name: Mordor Muster
key_examples.set_name: The Lord of the Rings: Tales of Middle-earth

mechanics.name: Basic Landcycling
mechanics.summary: Five commons (Eagles of the North, Lórien Revealed, Troll of Khazad-dûm, Oliphaunt, Generous Ent) cycle for a basic land. Enables mana flexibility and low land counts.
mechanics.performance: Essential utility — these commons play in nearly every deck.

general_tips: Play aggressive strategies that exploit the combat-heavy nature of the format.
Uncommon quality significantly exceeds common quality — follow premium signposts early.
Use landcyclers to safely reduce land count to 14-15 in fast decks.
Block skulk creatures with 1/3s and 0/3s — they're very predictable.
Black is the strongest color by a wide margin; don't pass open black signals.
Avoid green in isolation; commit only when strong Food/Elf uncommons or rares flow your way.
